required_skills: Systems Programming in C and C++
Operating Systems Internals and Kernel Development
Debugging with GDB and System-Level Tooling
responsibilities: Design and implement high-performance, low-latency systems software in C and C++ that operates efficiently under constrained memory and CPU budgets
Develop and maintain kernel modules, device drivers, or system daemons that interface directly with hardware components and OS subsystems
Architect inter-process communication mechanisms including shared memory, message queues, pipes, and Unix domain sockets for high-throughput data exchange
Profile and optimize system-level code using perf, Valgrind, and flame graphs to eliminate CPU hotspots and reduce memory allocation overhead
Implement thread-safe concurrent data structures and synchronization primitives to maximize multi-core utilization without deadlocks or race conditions
Debug complex system-level failures using GDB, strace, and core dump analysis to identify root causes in production and embedded environments
Design and validate memory management strategies including custom allocators, RAII patterns, and leak detection to ensure long-running process stability
Develop and review network protocol implementations at the socket level with strict attention to buffer management, flow control, and error recovery
Collaborate with hardware and firmware teams to define board support packages and hardware abstraction layers for new silicon platforms
Write detailed technical design documents and post-mortem analyses for complex system failures affecting critical infrastructure components
